texto: Decadent cooks go one step further and make sculptures of the food itself. If life is to be spent in pursuit of the extravagant, the extreme, the grotesque, the bizarre, then one's diet should reflect the fact. Life, meals, everything must be as artificial as possible - in fact works of art. So why not begin by eating a few statues?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Medlar Lucan que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “Decadent cooks go one step further and make sculptures of the food itself. If life is to be spent…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, cooking, decadence, com foco em trabalho e decadence.

frase_original: Decadent cooks go one step further and make sculptures of the food itself. If life is to be spent in pursuit of the extravagant, the extreme, the grotesque, the bizarre, then one's diet should reflect the fact. Life, meals, everything must be as artificial as possible - in fact works of art. So why not begin by eating a few statues?

texto: Another of the great civilizations, the Aztecs, raised a breed of hairless chihuahuas especially for eating. When the Conquistadors arrived and found dog on the menu, they were of the same opinion as Mademoiselle, that this was evidence of the worst form of barbarism. They, the Spaniards, used dogs as befits civilized and Christian men - to hunt down fugitive Indians and tear them to pieces.
explicacao: Citação de Medlar Lucan sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Another of the great civilizations, the Aztecs, raised a breed of hairless chihuahuas especially …” articula reflexao, aztecs, christians em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como christians e conquistador.
